scenario: Tessera Telco runs 30000 jobs per hour in Camunda 8.8. The job worker's average completion time spikes during peak hours, and the architect must set the activation timeout so a worker has 120 seconds to complete a job before the broker re-activates the job for another worker.
question: Which property sets the activation lock duration on a job activation in Camunda 8?

options.text: Set the activation timeout on the worker (or the request) to 120 seconds; the broker holds the job locked for the worker until the timeout elapses
Set the retries property on the service task to 120; the broker uses the retries field as a per-second timeout when the worker is connected
Set the assignee property to 120s on the user task; the broker reuses the assignee field as an activation timeout for adjacent service tasks
Set the custom header timeout=120 on the service task; the broker reads the header and treats the value as the activation timeout in seconds

a.text: Correct. Job activation specifies a lock timeout per call; the broker re-activates the job for another worker if the timeout elapses before completion.

b.text: Incorrect. Retries is a separate counter for failure recovery, not a per-activation timeout.

c.text: Incorrect. Assignee is a user task property and does not control job activation timeouts for service tasks.

d.text: Incorrect. Custom headers are static metadata; the timeout is a property of the activation request, not of the service task element.

explanation: Job activation in Camunda 8 takes a timeout per call; the broker re-activates the job if the worker does not complete it within that lock duration.
